rationale: The enforced governed-authorization control requires distinct requested_by and approved_by GitHub logins (self-approval refused). Sentinel Shield currently has a single admin/collaborator (bogdaniel) and no second human login, so the two-person identity control is structurally unsatisfiable and must not be met by fabricating a second identity or misusing an automation account. The release owner explicitly waived ONLY the two-person identity control for this specific candidate; the SSH-signed tag at the CI-proven source commit was created and pushed by the owner directly. See docs/release-authorization-policy.md.
scope_of_waiver: two-person requested_by != approved_by identity control ONLY; no other release gate is waived
